title: Login fails with OAuth tokens on mobile devices
body:
When using OAuth tokens on mobile devices, the login endpoint returns a 500 Internal Server Error.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Configure an OAuth 2.0 provider (tested with Google and GitHub)
2. Call the /auth/login endpoint with the OAuth access_token
3. Observe: HTTP 500 - Internal Server Error

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "src/auth/login.py", line 142, in authenticate
    token_data = session.get_oauth_token(request.headers["Authorization"])
  KeyError: 'access_token'

Environment:
- Python 3.10.4
- Ubuntu 22.04
- Library version: 2.3.1
- Tested via iOS API client and curl
author: mobile_dev_user

title: App crashes when I use it
body: The app just crashes. Please fix it.
author: vague_reporter

title: CRITICAL: Silent data loss when uploading files larger than 100MB
body:
This is a critical production bug causing data loss for enterprise customers.

When uploading files larger than 100MB through the API, the data gets silently truncated without any error message or warning. Users believe their data was saved successfully but it is actually corrupted.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a test file of 150MB
2. Upload via POST /api/v2/storage/upload
3. Download the file back
4. Compare checksums: they will not match

Traceback:
  File "src/storage/upload.py", line 89, in chunk_upload
    buffer = allocate_buffer(file_size)
  MemoryError: Unable to allocate buffer for file size 157286400

This is happening in production right now. Thousands of customers are affected.

Environment:
- Python 3.11.0
- Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
- Library version: 2.3.1
- Server: AWS EC2 t3.medium (4GB RAM)
author: enterprise_admin

title: Feature request: Add dark mode support to the dashboard UI
body:
It would be wonderful to have a dark mode option in the dashboard UI. Many developers prefer dark mode for long coding sessions, and it would significantly improve accessibility for users with certain visual sensitivities.

Suggested implementation: Use CSS custom properties (variables) for all color values so themes can be swapped at runtime. Many popular projects like VS Code and GitHub itself have implemented this successfully.

I'd be happy to contribute a PR if the maintainers agree this is worth adding. Could you add a toggle button in the user settings panel?
author: ui_enthusiast

title: Typo in README: 'recieve' should be 'receive'
body: Minor documentation fix needed. In the README.md file, the word 'recieve' is misspelled. It should be 'receive'. I believe it appears in the Contributing section near the top of the file. Worth fixing to maintain professionalism in the docs.
author: grammar_police
